title: rosa canina var. hispidula
intro: Rosa canina var. hispidula, also known as the dwarf wild rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small size and thorny branches. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. hispidula include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. hispidula can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health conditions, including diarrhea, dyspepsia, and skin irritations.
